challenges_encountered: Could not run tests due to .NET version mismatch in the environment
Needed to expose private _dbFactory as public property for use from Razor page
tips_for_future: Always check for .NET SDK compatibility before attempting to build/run tests
When views need database access, consider adding property accessors to services
Follow the existing pattern for async operations with proper StateHasChanged() usage
worked_well: Read the full source code of both modified files in single pass
Identified the need to expose DbContextFactory for database access from the view
Implemented the fix following existing patterns in the codebase

codebase_learnings: Services use Infrastructure.Inversion with IDbContextFactory for database access
BuildDetail page uses dependency injection for service access via @inject directive
The RefreshLogs method previously only simulated loading with Task.Delay - now it calls the actual API and persists to DB
The SweAfService has public properties for external access via dependency injection
Build notifier uses events (@property and += pattern for event subscription)
summary: Modified RefreshLogs method in BuildDetail.razor to call FetchExecutionDetailAsync and persist logs to database, and exposed DbContextFactory property in SweAfService
test_summary: Tests could not be run due to .NET SDK version mismatch (project targets .NET 10.0, but SDK available is 9.0.100)
